person.name: Sir William II de la Haye

person.alternateName: Sir William de Haya II, 1st Lord of Erroll, Butler of Scotland

sourceList.title: Legacy NFS Source: William de la Haye - Published information: birth-name: William de la Haye
William de la Haye of Errol - Butler of Scotland

events.name: known to have been in the Scottish court

William granted a charter to the Prior of St Andrews in which he and his wife, Eva, leased lands (eight carucates) in Pitmilly to the Priory of St Andrews for 20 years at an annual rent of half a mark of silver for the purposes of a hospital, meaning

William the Lion was captured at the 2nd Battle of Alnwick and taken to King Henry II in Falaise, Normandy. In order to regain his freedom, he had to sign the punitive Treaty of Falaise. One of the last provisions was that William the Lion had to send 21

The Treaty of Falaise was ratified at York and William the Lion and his brother David, having paid homage to Henry II of England for Scotland and Galloway, were allowed to return to Scotland. According to J. C. D. Hay, William de Haya

William the Lion granted Erroll (Herol), located on the north side of the Tay estuary, to William II de Haya for the service of two knights. The barony, which was granted as a hereditary right (in feu and heritage), awarded the privileges associated
He granted the lands of Ederpolls to Coupar Angus Abbey for the benefit of the souls of King Malcolm; his uncle Ranulf de Soulis, and others.
His grant of the lands of Ederpolls to Coupar Angus Abbey was confirmed by King William, although these dates are thought by Barrow to be too late.
William was one of the ambassadors sent by William the Lion to the newly crowned King John of England to try to have his lost patrimony of Northumberland and Cumberland returned to Scotland.
He was still alive, as proved by a charter of that date in the Benholm Charter-chest, but apparently died soon afterwards.

zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir William II de la Haye於1130年出生在法國諾門迪可騰時，他的父親William I de Haya31歲，母親Julianna de Soules10歲。他於1176年之前與Eva of Pitmilly在英國蘇格蘭結婚。他們至少是5個兒子的父母。他去世於1201年在英國蘇格蘭費伏享年71歲。

zh-hans.St Andrews, Fife, Scotland: 苏格兰費伏聖 安德魯斯
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir William II de la Haye于1130年出生在法国諾門迪可騰时，他的父亲William I de Haya31岁，母亲Julianna de Soules10岁。他于1176年之前与Eva of Pitmilly在英国苏格兰结婚。他们至少是5个儿子的父母。他去世于1201年在英国苏格兰費伏享年71岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: la
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Hung, Cuong, Anh, Thanh, Ha, Hoang, Tuan, Chau, Hao, Hien, Hoa, Hue, Nam, Phong, Sinh, Tuong, Min, Tam, Tanh, Thai, Tham, Tinh, Yeong.  Chinese Hong, Hang, Sang, Tong, Chan, Chi, Hwa Ja, Hyung, Kam, Ki Bong, Kin, Man.
Vietnamese: from the Chinese surname 羅, see   Luo   1.
Vietnamese (Lã): from the Chinese surname 呂, see Lu 6 and 3 below.

variants.name: Luo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Jian, Yi, Ping, Yong, Zhen, Feng, Hong, Ming, Jin, Bin, Chen, Hai, Lan, Min, Huan, Hung, Shen, Tian.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 羅, meaning ‘net for catching birds’ or ‘to collect’ in Chinese: (i) from Luo (羅), the name of an ancient state (located in present-day Hubei province). After it was annexed by the state of Chu during the early Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ), people from the state migrated southwards and adopted Luo (羅) as their surname. (ii) from the Po Duo Luo (破多羅) and Chi Luo (叱羅) families from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China, who changed their original surnames to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) from the personal name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅), leader of an ancient Turkic tribe who are believed to have migrated to China during the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ). His descendants inherited his name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅) as their surname and shortened it to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iv) from the name of one of the seven families from the Bandun Man, an ethnic group residing in ancient southwestern China (located mainly in present-day Sichuan province).

nameMeanings.name: Haye
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Hans, Helmut, Willi.

North German, East Frisian, and Dutch: from a personal name, Frisian Hajo, ancient Germanic Haio; or from a short form of the female name Hadewig, composed of the elements hadu ‘battle, strife’ + wīg ‘war’.
German: variant of   Hey   4, 5.

variants.name: Deshayes
meanings: French: topographic name for someone who lived by an enclosure, from the plural of Old French haye ‘hedge’ (see Haye and Hayes ), with fused preposition and definite article des ‘from the’; or a habitational name denoting someone from Les Hayes, the name of several places in various parts of France. Compare Deshaies .

variants.name: Hayenga
meanings: East Frisian: patronymic from the personal name Hajo (see Haye 1). It is formed with the suffix -enga, a variant of -inga (from the patronymic suffix -ing and genitive plural ending -a).

variants.name: Hey
meanings: English (Yorkshire and Lancashire): variant of   Hay  .
Dutch: variant, mostly Americanized (or archaic) and Flemish, of Heij, a topographic name for someone who lived on a heath, Dutch hei, heide.
German: metonymic occupational name for a grower or mower of grass, from Middle High German höu ‘grass, hay’.

variants.name: Hayen
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Alphonse.  German Heinz, Wilhelm.
Flemish, East Frisian, and North German: patronymic from   Haye   1.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

nameMeanings.name: II
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: 1  ♂ ‘Profit’ or ‘sharp’.
2  ♀, ♂ ‘Upright’.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir William II de la Haye was born in 1130, in Cotentin, Normandy, France, his father, William I de Haya, was 31 and his mother, Julianna de Soules, was 10. He married Eva of Pitmilly before 1176, in Scotland, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 5 sons. He died in 1201, in Fife, Scotland, United Kingdom, at the age of 71.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir William II de la Haye est né en 1130, à Cotentin, Normandie, France, son père, William I de Haya, avait 31 ans et sa mère, Julianna de Soules, avait 10 ans. Il a épousé Eva of Pitmilly avant 1176, à Écosse, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils. Il est décédé en 1201, à Fife, Écosse, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 71 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir William II de la Haye 1130 in Cotentin, Normandie, Frankreich geboren wurde, war sein Vater William I de Haya 31 und seine Mutter Julianna de Soules 10. Er hat vor 1176 in Schottland, United Kingdom Eva of Pitmilly geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne. Er starb 1201 im Alter von 71 Jahren in Fife, Schottland, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir William II de la Haye è nato il nel 1130 nel Cotentin, Normandie, Francia, il suo padre, William I de Haya, aveva 31 anni e il suo madre, Julianna de Soules, aveva 10 anni. Lui sposato Eva of Pitmilly prima del 1176, nel Scozia, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli. Morì nel 1201, nel Fife, Scozia, Regno Unito, all'età di 71 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir William II de la Hayeが1130年にフランスノーマンディコテンティンで生まれた時，父William I de Hayaは31歳，母Julianna de Soulesは10歳であった。1176年以前にイギリススコットランドでEva of Pitmillyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子の親であった。彼は1201年にイギリススコットランドフィフで71歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir William II de la Haye(이)가 1130년 프랑스 노먼디 컷틴에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 William I de Haya(은)는 31세였고, 그의 어머니 Julianna de Soules(은)는 10세였다. 그는 1176년 이전 영국 스코틀랜드에서 Eva of Pitmilly(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1201년 영국 스코틀랜드 피프에서 71세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir William II de la Haye nasceu em 1130, em Cotentin, Normandie, França, seu pai, William I de Haya, tinha 31 anos e sua mãe, Julianna de Soules, tinha 10 anos. Ele casou-se com Eva of Pitmilly antes 1176, em Escócia, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos. Ele faleceu em 1201, em Fife, Escócia, United Kingdom, com 71 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir William II de la Haye родился в 1130 г. в местности котентинь, норманди, Франция, его отцу по имени William I de Haya было 31 год, а матери, которую звали Julianna de Soules, 10. Он женился на женщине по имени Eva of Pitmilly перед 1176 г. в местности Шотландия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей. Он умер в 1201 г. в местности фиф, Шотландия, Великобритания в возрасте 71 год.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir William II de la Haye nació en 1130, en Cotentin, Normandie, Francia, su papá, William I de Haya, tenía 31 años y su mamá, Julianna de Soules, tenía 10 años. Se casó con Eva of Pitmilly antes de 1176, en Escocia, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos. Murió en 1201, en Fife, Escocia, Reino Unido, a la edad de 71 años.
